Tip: # starts a comment not a prompt
Tip: Put CLAUDE.md in your repo root
Tip: /init creates a CLAUDE.md for you
Tip: claude -p runs headless pipe mode
Tip: echo prompt | claude pipes in
Tip: Up arrow recalls your last prompt
Tip: claude update updates the CLI
Tip: Custom commands are just .md files
Tip: Ctrl+U deletes the whole line
Tip: Ctrl+W deletes the last word
Tip: Ctrl+A jumps to start of line
Tip: Ctrl+E jumps to end of line
Tip: I can read images just paste them
Tip: I can read PDFs too
Tip: I auto-compact when context gets full
Tip: hooks run commands on my actions
Tip: PostToolUse hooks fire after each tool
Tip: PreToolUse hooks can block tools
Tip: SessionStart hooks set up your env
Tip: I can run multiple tools in parallel
Tip: /permissions manages what I can do
Tip: Mention a file path and I'll read it
Tip: I track my own working directory
Tip: git diff before commit shows what changed
Tip: I can create PRs with gh
Tip: Ask me to plan before I code
Tip: Plan mode prevents wasted effort
Tip: I can spawn subagents for parallel work
Tip: Explore agents search your codebase fast
Tip: Agent teams coordinate multiple Claudes
Tip: 3-5 teammates is the sweet spot
Tip: I can read Jupyter notebooks
Tip: I can edit notebook cells individually
Tip: statusLine shows custom info below
Tip: mode:append keeps defaults plus yours
Tip: Chord bindings like Ctrl+K Ctrl+P work
Tip: I have LSP for go-to-definition
Tip: I can find all references of a symbol
Tip: I can show hover docs for any symbol
Tip: Pipe my output to other commands
Tip: claude -p < file.txt reads file as prompt
Tip: --model picks which Claude to use
Tip: Opus is smartest Haiku is fastest
Tip: Sonnet balances speed and smarts
Tip: I see your terminal width and adjust
Tip: Long files I can read in chunks
Tip: I can glob for **/*.tsx patterns
Tip: I can regex search with Grep
Tip: MCP servers add external tools
Tip: Connect me to Notion Slack Gmail etc
Tip: WebSearch lets me Google things
Tip: WebFetch reads any public URL
Tip: I can read screenshots you paste
Tip: I preserve file permissions when editing
Tip: Edit tool does exact string replacement
Tip: Write tool creates or overwrites files
Tip: I backup nothing so commit often
Tip: I can run dev servers in background
Tip: Background tasks free up the main thread
Tip: I won't push unless you tell me to
Tip: I won't commit unless you tell me to
Tip: I check git status before committing
Tip: I match your repo's commit style
Tip: HEREDOC format keeps commit msgs clean
Tip: I add Co-Authored-By on commits
Tip: gh pr create makes PRs from terminal
Tip: I generate PR descriptions automatically
Tip: /review-pr reviews any PR for you
Tip: I can comment on GitHub issues
Tip: Put project conventions in CLAUDE.md
Tip: One CLAUDE.md per directory works
Tip: Nested CLAUDE.md files stack up
Tip: I sandbox Bash by default
Tip: Permission modes control my autonomy
Tip: allowEdits mode lets me edit freely
Tip: default mode asks before risky stuff
Tip: I can run tests and fix what fails
Tip: Give me your test command in CLAUDE.md
Tip: I can debug by reading stack traces
Tip: Paste an error and I'll investigate
Tip: I check OWASP top 10 automatically
Tip: I avoid command injection in Bash
Tip: I escape user input in generated code
Tip: I won't force push to main ever
Tip: I won't reset --hard without asking
Tip: Old messages get summarized to save space
Tip: Your conversation has no hard limit
Tip: Shorter prompts save input tokens
Tip: Be specific to get better results
Tip: File paths help me find things faster
Tip: Line numbers help me edit precisely
Tip: I show file:line for easy navigation
Tip: I can handle multiple files at once
Tip: Parallel tool calls are faster
Tip: I read before I edit always
Tip: I prefer editing over creating files
Tip: I don't over-engineer your code
Tip: I keep changes minimal and focused
Tip: I don't add comments unless needed
Tip: I match your existing code style
Tip: npx tweakcc customizes the spinner star
Tip: Custom themes change spinner colors
Tip: SessionEnd hooks clean up temp files
Tip: jq is your friend for JSON in scripts
Tip: Status line scripts receive JSON on stdin
Tip: total_input_tokens counts all tokens in
Tip: total_output_tokens counts all tokens out
Tip: git rev-parse finds repo root
Tip: symbolic-ref --short HEAD gets branch name
Tip: Scope temp files with PID to avoid conflicts
Tip: hooks timeout after 5 seconds by default
Tip: matcher filters which tools trigger hooks
Tip: You're reading tip number who cares
FYI: Code is just text files telling computers what to do
FYI: A bug is when code does something you didn't want
FYI: A feature is a bug your boss likes
FYI: Variables store stuff like x = 5
FYI: A string is just text in quotes like 'hello'
FYI: An integer is a whole number no decimals
FYI: A float is a number with a decimal point
FYI: A boolean is just true or false that's it
FYI: An array is a list of things [1, 2, 3]
FYI: An object is a thing with named properties
FYI: A function is a reusable chunk of code
FYI: Arguments are what you pass into a function
FYI: Return is what a function gives back to you
FYI: A loop repeats code until you tell it to stop
FYI: An infinite loop never stops and that's bad
FYI: If/else is how code makes decisions
FYI: == checks if things are equal
FYI: = assigns a value == compares values
FYI: && means AND both must be true
FYI: || means OR either can be true
FYI: ! means NOT it flips true to false
FYI: null means nothing exists there on purpose
FYI: undefined means nobody set a value yet
FYI: NaN means Not a Number ironically it's a number type
FYI: An API is how programs talk to each other
FYI: REST is a way to structure API calls
FYI: GET fetches data POST sends data
FYI: PUT updates data DELETE removes data
FYI: JSON is how the internet passes data around
FYI: CSS makes it look pretty
FYI: JavaScript makes it do stuff
FYI: The DOM is how the browser sees your HTML
FYI: A div is just a box on a webpage
FYI: A class in CSS is a reusable style
FYI: A class in code is a blueprint for objects
FYI: Inheritance means one class copies another
FYI: A method is a function that belongs to an object
FYI: A constructor runs when you create a new object
FYI: A library is someone else's code you borrow
FYI: A framework is a library that tells you what to do
FYI: brew installs Mac apps from terminal
FYI: A package is just code someone shared online
FYI: node_modules is where npm puts packages
FYI: node_modules is also 90% of your hard drive
FYI: Dependencies are packages your code needs
FYI: A dependency tree is packages that need packages
FYI: Dependency hell is when they all fight each other
FYI: Git tracks every change you make to code
FYI: A commit is a save point in git
FYI: A branch is a parallel version of your code
FYI: Main/master is the official branch
FYI: Merging combines two branches together
FYI: A merge conflict is when two edits fight
FYI: Pull means download changes from the server
FYI: Push means upload your changes to the server
FYI: A repo is just a folder tracked by git
FYI: GitHub is where repos live online
FYI: A PR is asking to merge your branch into main
FYI: Code review is someone checking your work
FYI: The terminal is a text-only way to control your computer
FYI: The shell interprets what you type in terminal
FYI: Bash and zsh are types of shells
FYI: A path is the address of a file like /Users/you/file.txt
FYI: .. means go up one folder
FYI: ls lists files in a folder
FYI: cd changes which folder you're in
FYI: mkdir makes a new folder
FYI: rm deletes a file forever no trash can
FYI: rm -rf deletes everything and asks no questions
FYI: sudo means do this as admin aka god mode
FYI: chmod changes who can read/write/run a file
FYI: A script is just a file full of terminal commands
FYI: An environment variable is a system-wide setting
FYI: PATH tells your computer where to find programs
FYI: $HOME is your home folder as a variable
FYI: A process is a running program
FYI: PID is a process ID every running thing has one
FYI: kill stops a process by its PID
FYI: Ctrl+C kills whatever's running in terminal
FYI: A server is just a computer that waits for requests
FYI: Localhost is your own computer acting as a server
FYI: Port 3000 is where dev servers usually live
FYI: Port 443 is HTTPS port 80 is HTTP
FYI: HTTP is how browsers talk to servers
FYI: HTTPS is HTTP but encrypted so nobody snoops
FYI: A cookie is data a website stores on your computer
FYI: A token is a temporary password for APIs
FYI: Authentication is proving who you are
FYI: Authorization is proving what you're allowed to do
FYI: Encryption scrambles data so only the right person reads it
FYI: Hashing turns data into a fixed-length fingerprint
FYI: You can't un-hash a hash that's the point
FYI: SQL is how you talk to databases
FYI: A database is a structured place to store data
FYI: A table is like a spreadsheet in a database
FYI: A row is one record in a table
FYI: A column is one field like name or email
FYI: A query asks the database a question
FYI: SELECT gets data INSERT adds data
FYI: UPDATE changes data DELETE removes data
FYI: SQL injection is when hackers type SQL into your form
FYI: XSS is when hackers inject scripts into your page
FYI: CORS is the browser blocking cross-site requests
FYI: Everyone hates CORS until they need it
FYI: A 200 means OK everything worked
FYI: A 404 means the page doesn't exist
FYI: A 500 means the server broke internally
FYI: A 403 means you're not allowed
FYI: A 401 means you're not logged in
FYI: A 418 means I'm a teapot (real HTTP code)
FYI: A 301 means this page moved permanently
FYI: A 503 means the server is overwhelmed
FYI: Async means don't wait do other stuff meanwhile
FYI: Sync means wait here until this finishes
FYI: A callback is a function that runs after another finishes
FYI: A promise is a callback that's easier to read
FYI: Async/await makes promises look like normal code
FYI: Callback hell is nested callbacks 10 levels deep
FYI: An event listener waits for something to happen
FYI: Click scroll keypress are all events
FYI: The console is where errors show up in the browser
FYI: console.log prints stuff to the console for debugging
FYI: A stack trace shows you where the error happened
FYI: Read the error message it usually tells you what's wrong
FYI: Most errors are typos honestly
FYI: A semicolon ends a statement in most languages
FYI: Python doesn't use semicolons it uses indentation
FYI: Indentation matters in Python mess it up and it breaks
FYI: TypeScript is JavaScript but it checks your types
FYI: Types prevent you from adding a number to a sandwich
FYI: Compiling turns human code into computer code
FYI: Interpreting runs code line by line no compiling needed
FYI: Python is interpreted JavaScript is kinda both
FYI: A linter checks your code style like a grammar teacher
FYI: A formatter auto-fixes your code style for you
FYI: Prettier formats JavaScript ESLint checks for errors
FYI: A test checks if your code does what you think it does
FYI: Unit tests check one small piece at a time
FYI: Integration tests check if pieces work together
FYI: CI/CD automatically tests and deploys your code
FYI: Docker puts your app in a container so it runs anywhere
FYI: The cloud is just someone else's computer
FYI: AWS Azure and GCP are the big three clouds
FYI: Serverless still uses servers you just don't manage them
FYI: DNS turns domain names into IP addresses
FYI: An IP address is your computer's phone number
FYI: localhost is 127.0.0.1 it's your own machine
FYI: SSH lets you control a remote computer from terminal
FYI: A regex is a pattern for matching text and it's unreadable
FYI: Nobody actually understands regex they just Google it
FYI: Stack Overflow is where programmers copy code from
FYI: Now they copy from ChatGPT instead
FYI: Open source means anyone can see and use the code
FYI: A license tells you what you can do with open source code
FYI: MIT license means do whatever you want
FYI: GPL means share your changes back
FYI: Refactoring is rewriting code without changing what it does
FYI: Tech debt is the mess you'll clean up 'later'
FYI: Later never comes for tech debt
FYI: DRY means Don't Repeat Yourself
FYI: KISS means Keep It Simple Stupid
FYI: YAGNI means You Ain't Gonna Need It so don't build it
FYI: Rubber duck debugging is explaining the bug to a duck
FYI: It works because saying it out loud helps you think
FYI: I'm your rubber duck now
